should_trigger: Design a MAPE-K control loop for our agent orchestrator so it can detect tool failures, diagnose the likely cause, plan a remediation, and execute it safely.
Our runtime has ad hoc retries, alerts, and model reroutes. Turn this into an autonomic self-healing and self-optimization architecture with shared knowledge.
Audit this auto-orchestrator for self-management: what should Monitor, Analyze, Plan, Execute, and Knowledge each contain, and where are the unsafe effectors?
We need an agent system that can self-protect when evaluators flag unsafe behavior, quarantine risky tools, and escalate only when the policy requires it.
should_not_trigger: Summarize the history of IBM autonomic computing for a lecture slide.
Create a Grafana dashboard that displays API latency and error rate, but do not design automated remediation.
Explain how the autonomic nervous system works in biology.
edge_cases: We need better monitoring for our orchestrator. Trigger this skill only if the monitoring feeds an autonomous Analyze-Plan-Execute control loop; otherwise use observability guidance.

We want agents to coordinate with each other. Use this skill if the coordination is for self-management of runtime resources; use multiagent-system patterns if the request is mainly about negotiation or communication protocols.
